---
title: "Lectures"
subtitle: "BMC201 - Web Technology"
---

## Course Lectures

This folder contains all lectures for BMC201 - Web Technology course.

### Week 1: Introduction to Web Development
- [Lecture 1: Introduction to Web Page Designing & Web Development Strategies](lecture-01.qmd)
- [Lecture 2: History of Web & Internet, Protocols Governing the Web](lecture-02.qmd)
- [Lecture 3: HTML Introduction & Basic Structure, HTML Tags](lecture-03.qmd)
- [Lecture 4: HTML Grouping: Div & Span](lecture-04.qmd)
- [Practice Quiz Set (Lectures 1-4)](/quiz/index.qmd)

### Week 2: HTML Forms & CSS
- [Lecture 5: HTML Lists (Ordered, Unordered, Description)](lecture-05.qmd)
- [Lecture 6: HTML Images & Hyperlinks](lecture-06.qmd)
- [Lecture 7: HTML Tables & Iframes](lecture-07.qmd)
- [Lecture 8: HTML Forms & Form Elements](lecture-08.qmd)

### Week 3: CSS Foundations
- [Lecture 9: CSS Introduction & CSS Syntax](lecture-09.qmd)
- [Lecture 10: External Style Sheets, Multiple Style Sheets](lecture-10.qmd)
- [Lecture 11: CSS Value Lengths, Percentages, Selectors](lecture-11.qmd)
- [Lecture 12: CSS Box Model, Float & Clear](lecture-12.qmd)

### Week 4: Bootstrap & JavaScript Basics
- [Lecture 13: Introduction to Bootstrap](lecture-13.qmd)
- [Lecture 14: Revision & Practical Exercise (HTML + CSS + Bootstrap)](lecture-14.qmd)
- [Lecture 15: Introduction to JavaScript & Variables](lecture-15.qmd)
- [Lecture 16: Functions in JavaScript & Returning Data](lecture-16.qmd)
- [Practice Quiz Set (Lectures 13-16)](/quiz/index.qmd)

### Week 5: JavaScript Control & Objects
- [Lecture 17: UI Events & Event Handling](lecture-17.qmd)
- [Lecture 18: Conditions & Looping in JavaScript](lecture-18.qmd)
- [Lecture 19: Block Scope Variables (let, const)](lecture-19.qmd)
- [Lecture 20: Objects & Object Literals](lecture-20.qmd)
- [Practice Quiz Set (Lectures 17-20)](/quiz/index.qmd)

### Weeks 6-7: Mid-Semester Exam Preparation
- [Revision: Unit I - Web Page Designing](revision-unit-1.qmd)
- [Revision: Unit II - JavaScript](revision-unit-2.qmd)

### Week 8: JavaScript DOM to Servlet Introduction
- [Lecture 21: DOM Manipulation using JavaScript](lecture-21.qmd)
- [Lecture 22: Revision & Mini Project (JavaScript & DOM)](lecture-22.qmd)
- [Lecture 23: Servlet Overview & Architecture](lecture-23.qmd)
- [Lecture 24: Servlet Interface & Life Cycle](lecture-24.qmd)

### Week 9: Servlet HTTP & Session Management
- [Lecture 25: Handling HTTP GET & POST Requests](lecture-25.qmd)
- [Lecture 26: Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching](lecture-26.qmd)
- [Lecture 27: Cookies & Session Tracking](lecture-27.qmd)
- [Lecture 28: Session Tracking using HttpSession](lecture-28.qmd)

### Week 10: JSP Core & Practical Revision
- [Lecture 29: JSP Introduction & JSP Overview](lecture-29.qmd)
- [Lecture 30: JSP Implicit Objects & Scripting](lecture-30.qmd)
- [Lecture 31: JSP Directives, Standard Actions & Custom Tag Libraries](lecture-31.qmd)
- [Lecture 32: Revision & Practical (Servlets + JSP)](lecture-32.qmd)

### Week 11: Spring Core
- [Lecture 33: Spring Core Basics & Dependency Injection](lecture-33.qmd)
- [Lecture 34: Design Patterns: Factory & Strategy](lecture-34.qmd)
- [Lecture 35: Spring IoC, Bean Scopes & Life Cycle Callbacks](lecture-35.qmd)
- [Lecture 36: AOP, Autowiring, Annotations, Bean Configuration Styles](lecture-36.qmd)
- [Practice Quiz Set (Lectures 33-36)](/quiz/index.qmd)

### Week 12: Spring Boot & REST Services
- [Lecture 37: Revision & Hands-on (Spring Core)](lecture-37.qmd)
- [Lecture 38: Spring Boot Configuration, Annotations & Build Systems](lecture-38.qmd)
- [Lecture 39: RESTful Web Services: Controllers, Request Mapping, CRUD APIs](lecture-39.qmd)
- [Lecture 40: Spring Boot Actuator, Logger, Web App Build & Final Revision](lecture-40.qmd)
- [Practice Quiz Set (Lectures 37-40)](/quiz/index.qmd)

---

## How to Use These Lectures

1. Click on any lecture link to view the lecture source
2. To present, render the Quarto file to RevealJS format
3. Use arrow keys to navigate slides
4. Press 'S' for speaker notes
5. Press 'F' for fullscreen

## Styling

All lectures use the custom SCSS theme from [course-slides-styles.scss](../course-slides-styles.scss), which is based on the UCSB-MEDS presentation template.
